message:
[MEDIUM] fcp-crypto+fuzz: adversarial COSE parser fuzz + HKDF/COSE metamorphic tests

- fuzz_cose_parse_adversarial: raw-bytes parser fuzz for CoseToken::from_cbor;
  asserts byte-stable re-encode across parse→to_cbor→reparse, and that verify
  paths never panic on arbitrary input (covers the tightened crit/alg preflight
  from 6d185b6b which previously had no negative fuzz coverage).
- hkdf.rs: three new metamorphic tests — mutual distinctness across the full
  SessionDirection × session_id product, mutual distinctness across every
  MacKeyPurpose variant, and a swap-asymmetry check proving direction labels
  actually contribute to the derivation (guards against label-table drift).
- cose.rs: single-byte-flip tamper-detection metamorphic test over the
  encoded token; every 1/16th-stride mutation must either fail to parse or
  fail to verify. Fills a gap — cose.rs had zero negative signature tests.

Verified: cargo test -p fcp-crypto --lib → 513 passed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -49,6 +49,13 @@ test = false
 doc = false
 bench = false
 
+[[bin]]
+name = "fuzz_cose_parse_adversarial"
+path = "fuzz_targets/cose_parse_adversarial.rs"
+test = false
+doc = false
+bench = false
+
 [[bin]]
 name = "fuzz_session"
 path = "fuzz_targets/session.rs"
